Title: Unsafe Space Garden
Location:
Elysium
705 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Unsafe Space Garden is a genre-bending outfit from Portugal known for their chaotic, theatrical and deeply immersive live shows. With a sound rooted in neo-psychedelia, experimental rock and a touch of surreal humour, the band pushes musical boundaries with fearless creativity and a clear artistic vision.

Described by European press as “a surrealist orchestra,” their concerts often blur the line between performance and ritual - unpredictable, cathartic and strangely uplifting. Their critically acclaimed albums Bro, You Got Something In Your Eye – A Guided Meditation (2021) and Where’s The Ground? (2023) draw comparisons to artists like Frank Zappa, Captain Beefheart or The Flaming Lips, while forging a language uniquely their own.

More than just experimentalists, Unsafe Space Garden are storytellers - unpredictable, emotional and sonically adventurous. A live act designed to be seen, heard, and remembered.

Title: Commercial Breaks
Location:
Chess Club
617 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Tight, catchy, succinct, and loud are the best words for Commercial Breaks. Formed in 2025 by a gaggle of rockers and co-conspirators, the group features Lucas Martins, Trey Gutierrez and David Rawlinson, all of whom back Gus Baldwin as “The Sketch” as well as Doran Rawlinson, former drummer of Nolan Potter’s Nightmare Band. The members have performed around Austin for many years but never in such a hook-driven, song-forward capacity. Their debut record out Q4 2025 was recorded entirely at home to cassette tape to ensure absolute commercial viability, and their live shows pursue the title of either Austin’s “Loudest Pop Group” or “Pop-est Loud Group”.

Title: Gus Baldwin & The Sketch
Location:
Chess Club
617 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Gus Baldwin is a singer/songwriter/ex-hippie/part-time punk from Austin, TX. After a stint holding down drums for Dallas surf punks Sealion in high school, Baldwin went on to form the Psych Pop combo Acid Carousel, which he led for 6 years and 20 something releases before departing the band. Moving to Austin, Baldwin started quickly self releasing a string of singles and EPs on his own GetWithIt! Imprint and establishing himself as the godson of Texas Rock n Roll. Teaming up with former bandmates and friends Gus formed The Sketch in 2022. Embracing the aggression of dirty punk, the looseness of garage, and the charm of power pop, The Sketch established themselves as new cornerstones of the Austin rock scene. Recording their debut LP with the group in one day of recording in LA in the spring of 2024, “The Sketch” is the culmination of Baldwin’s vision, manifested through the power of The Sketch themselves. In late 2025, Baldwin released his first full length solo LP. With the selt-titled debut, Gus focuses in on his rock n' roll stylings, while at the same time showing great versatility as a songwriter. Not being afraid to mix opposite loves of Punk and Pop into one cohesive album.

Title: Spacestation
Location:
Zilker Brewing
1701 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702

Title: Jazz re:freshed DJs
Location:
Flamingo Cantina
515 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
As a close-knit family made up of music promoters, DJs, label owners and festival organisers, they’ve been regenerating jazz and jazz-influenced music culture since their 2003 conception.
JRF has cooked up a session that brings the spirit of the jazz & club culture they hail from.

THE DJs
Top Rock and Adam Rock - Founders of Jazz re:freshed

Title: Bikini Beach
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: No description provided for film.

Title: fifi knifefight
Location:
Chess Club
617 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: fifi knifefight is an Austin based glam punk band known for a powerful live presence and raw sound. Blending punk grit with theatrical flair, this is a must-see act for fans of Hole. Fronted by dynamic lead, the eponymous fifi knifefight, their performances often feature driving rhythms, unapologetic lyrics, and an in-your-face style that's intense yet captivating. Tracks like "joanie" and "THE PARTY" showcase their unique mix of emotional depth and defiance. Often found in downtown Austin and recently featured in several local festivals, fifi knifefight continues to make waves in the indie circuits.

Title: Gummy Fang
Location:
Chess Club
617 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Austin, Texas’ Gummy Fang released their self titled debut album in July of 2025 featuring jangly, uptempo indie pop anthems and textural introspective ballads. The Austin Chronicle named it their 7th favorite local album of the year and nominated the band for Best New Act at the annual Austin Music Award. Gummy Fang is known for their cheery onstage charm, heartfelt crooning, intricate countermelodies on bass, and drumming that combines jazz vocabulary with a meaty rock pulse.

Singer, guitarist, and songwriter Willow Braun pens infectious love songs which are accompanied by winding, classical inspired bass counterpoint by Fructose, and nuanced, in the pocket, jazz inspired drumming from Nicky Christmas.

Title: Kanho Yakushiji
Location:
Elysium
705 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: No description provided for film.

Title: CJ CHENIER AND THE RED HOT LOUISIANA BAND
Location:
Antone’s
305 E 5th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: CJ Chenier is born and raised in an indigenous American culture with its distinct language, cuisine and music. Chenier, a Grammy nominated world renowned musician and recording artist, has performed alongside many internationally recognized artists including his GRAMMY awarded winning father, Clifton Chenier “The King of Zydeco,” Paul Simon, Joe Sample, and Ray Parker, Jr. He appeared on the Jon Stewart Show, CNN, was featured on VH1, and Entertainment Weekly.  He has exhibited his musicianship at many large festival venues such as the New Orleans Jazz and Heritage Festival, Chicago Blues Festival, and Austin SxSW Music Conference. In addition, to a GRAMMY award nomination in the Best Regional Roots Music Album category, C.J has received many major awards inclusive of the Living Blues Critics’Poll Award, India Award (from AFIM) for Best Zydeco Album, Coups de Coeur (Blows of Heart) Blues Category Award from I’Academie Charles Cros (France) and Best of the Beat Best Zydeco Album (Offbeat Magazine).

Title: Pink Breath of Heaven
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: No description provided for film.

Title: Water Damage
Location:
The 13th Floor
711 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
“Arguably the most exciting rock band to emerge from the American experimental underground this decade.” - Grayson Haver Currin, MOJO Magazine 2025

Drones are a powerful force, capable of whisking our thinking mind far, far, away. Austin-based collective Water Damage yields the same power whenever they pick up their instruments. Veterans from other projects (a.o. Swans, more eaze, Spray Paint, USA/Mexico and more), the band is not unfamiliar with the unshakeable power in drones; still, as Water Damage, they stretch out drone’s possibilities to their limits, bowing to the principle ‘Maximal Repetition Minimal Deviation’. Water Damage explore ecstatic oblivion, repeating basic riffs and rhythms until time seems to falter. Once an idea takes off, they pursue it forever; as Pitchfork said, their 2024 double LP ‘In E’ doubles as a “glorious mind eraser”.

Title: haha Laughing
Location:
Chess Club
617 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: haha Laughing is an Austin-based experimental noise rap project consisting of long-time local musicians Jay Dilick and Aby Oviedo. The duo blends elements of hardcore rap, free-jazz, distorted electronic music and punk energy into 3 minute explosions of catchy catharsis. The chaos they curate is driven home through their live shows where Dilick and Oviedo's back and forth vocals, noise breakdowns, sample flips, and sense of playfulness create a must see live performance that is energetically engaging and delightfully disorienting. haha Laughing has released multiple EP's and singles since 2021, and released their first album "LOSING TEETH DREAM" in May of 2025. Their follow up EP "BIG WHATEVER" is set to be released Spring 2026. haha Live. haha Laugh. haha Love.

Title: TTSSFU
Location:
Swan Dive
Swan Dive Patio
615 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: TTSSFU – aka Tasmin Nicole Stephens – quickly went from being a mere fixture in Manchester’s indie scene to one of its fastest-rising stars, now poised to break out well beyond it. Known for conjuring vast, atmospheric worlds from her bedroom using just Garageband, she watched as early releases like her Warhol-inspired 2024 EP “Me, Jed and Andy” picked up steam. Since then, she’s traded bedrooms for stages supporting Kim Deal, English Teacher, Soccer Mommy and Mannequin Pussy, plus sold out headlines across the UK and packed festival sets from Green Man to the Great Escape.

Title: Pedal Steel Noah
Location:
The 13th Floor
711 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
18-year-old Austin-based musician Noah Faulkner has captivated audiences with daily Instagram videos featuring his pedal steel covers of ‘80s and ‘90s hits, often joined by his brother Nate on bass and their dog Kara.

Noah's fan base boasts legends like R.E.M., Johnny Marr, Peter Hook, Frank Black, Neko Case, Duran Duran, and more. Over the past year, he has played onstage with the Polyphonic Spree and Modest Mouse, toured the country with his band, and was featured in The New York Times for his rising influence.

In August 2024, Noah made his Grand Ole Opry debut, invited by Vince Gill to perform Buck Owens' classic “Together Again.” Despite never playing it before, he delivered a flawless rendition, earning a standing ovation for his unforgettable performance.

Title: J’cuuzi
Location:
Swan Dive
Swan Dive Patio
615 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Describing J’cuuzi as a band is at the very least inaccurate, or worse: incorrect. While they deliver electrifying, borderline acrobatic live performances of high octane, glitchy songs that modulate between serrated post punk guitar and pulsing electro beats, there is a shocking theatricality to them that the word “band” lacks the range to capture. The members wear handmade couture, with bulbous anime-esque proportions and insectile features sewn into drag silhouettes. They often perform while stacked on top of each other, or hoisted over each other’s heads. Their stage is set like a cheap studio apartment: a spinning blue egg chair and a stack of guitar amps sit on either side of a bedazzled ironing board, from which a spinning disco ball hangs. Since their inception in February 2024, J’cuuzi’s ferocity has earned them opening slots for acts like Viagra Boys, Stereolab, Tune-Yards, Shannon and the Clams, Upchuck, Snooper, and Warmduscher. Whether you call them a band, a performance art troupe, or a fashion house; J’cuuzi has set a pace of expansion and experimentation that proves theirs is an act worth watching.

Title: Joyeria
Location:
Swan Dive
615 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Joyeria (pronounced Joy-ah-ree-ah) is a London-based outfit led by a Canadian-Polish songwriter who famously spent years quitting bands at their peak to keep his creative soul intact. Now a staple of London's underground scene, the group is a vehicle for "working in the dark"—a shape-shifting project that prizes sincerity over polish.

Musically, Joyeria delivers a darkly comic blend of slacker indie and experimental post-punk. Fronted by a deadpan baritone, the songs tackle the absurdity of modern adulthood, capitalism, and workday fatigue with biting, sardonic wit. Their EPs, FIM and Graceful Degradation, were produced by Dan Carey, captured raw and live-to-tape to preserve the "chaos" of the moment rather than a rehearsed demo.

While born from a singular vision, Joyeria is a formidable four-piece band. Known for being "purposely under-rehearsed," the ensemble thrives on improvisation, ensuring no two sets are the same. With a stage presence that balances Ian Curtis’s intensity with Stephen Malkmus’s irony, the band utilizes an unpredictable, dissonant energy to create a sound that is as imposing as it is eccentric.

Title: Alien Chicks
Location:
Swan Dive
615 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Alien Chicks are a trio hailing from the streets of South London, known for their electrifying blend of punk, rap and jazz. They create a sound that is raw and innovative, capturing the spirit of London's underground music culture. The line-up features the vocals and sharp guitar work of Josef Lindsay, the pulsating basslines of Stefan Parker-Steele and the explosive drumming of Martha Daniels. Whether delivering rapid-fire rap verses, intricate jazz-inspired bass riffs, or thunderous punk rhythms, Alien Chicks continue to push boundaries and defy expectations, drawing praise from The Independent, NME, Rolling Stone, and Radio 1.

The band’s journey began in grassroots venues across the city, in particular The Windmill, Brixton, where their high-energy performances and unique sound helped them build a loyal following. With huge momentum following their biggest London headliner yet at The Garage, following a sold out Oslo Hackney, four iconic sets at The Great Escape, 2000 Trees and Glastonbury. They have also toured with English Teacher, Lambrini Girls, The Libertines and Big Special.

Alien Chicks' music is a testament to their diverse influences and fearless approach to genre.

Title: Whitelands
Location:
Swan Dive
615 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Originally inspired by a Slowdive KEXP session, London-based quartet Whitelands fuse their diverse musical backgrounds into an updated spin on shoegaze that amplifies marginalized voices and perspectives. Formed in the late 2010s as the solo project of Etienne Quartey-Papafio, Whitelands evolved into a full band ahead of their official debut album, Night-Bound Eyes Are Blind to the Day (2024), released during a U.K. tour with Slowdive. The project takes its name from a campus at London’s Roehampton University, where Quartey-Papafio first performed live. Their self-titled 2018 debut, a lo-fi blend of jagged indie rock and hazy grunge textures, hinted at the immersive sound they would later embrace. A turning point came when Quartey-Papafio stumbled upon Slowdive’s 2017 KEXP performance, igniting a deeper fixation on shoegaze. The current lineup consists of Jagun Meseorisa, Vanessa Govinden, and Michael Adelaja who bonded through shared experiences of neurodivergence and racial identity. Whitelands’ forthcoming album, Sunlight Echoes (out January 30, 2026), expands their sound, moving beyond shoegaze’s shadows toward something bigger, brighter, and unmistakably their own.

Title: bloodsports
Location:
Hotel Vegas
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Taking over sweaty New York venues and curbside congregations, bloodsports has become an undeniable force with their latest lineup—Sam Murphy (guitar/vocals), Jeremy Mock (guitar), Liv Eriksen (bass/vocals), and Scott Hale (drums)—finding solace in sonic strain where angular sedition collides with economical melodies that ease the weight of self-destruction. Their long-awaited debut album Anything Can Be A Hammer marks a bold evolution, embracing a more dynamic, mature approach as the band balances experimentation and curiosity with rhythmic blows and emotional depth; fragile yet abundant, the record leans into patience and interior tension, with Murphy’s lyricism blurring the line between what we know and what we think we know—it’s an album with blood in its veins and a finger on the pulse. The release quickly caught fire with praise from Stereogum, Vice, Alternative Press, Consequence, and more, leading to a support slot on They Are Gutting A Body of Water’s sold-out album release show and performances alongside Starcleaner Reunion and Brutus VII. In 2026, the Brooklyn four-piece is set to take their hard-hitting live show to SXSW, across the country, and around the world.

Title: Grocery Bag
Location:
Zilker Brewing
1701 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702
Description: Grocery Bag are the latest in a new breed of face-melting rock & roll straight out of Austin, Texas. The garage rock-psych-punk 4 piece, led by diminutive singer Bella Martinez, have been tearing up stages around Texas since 2023, being handpicked by the likes of Osees, Frankie & The Witch Fingers & Farmer's Wife to support on multiple shows as well as a critically-acclaimed set at Levitation Festival 2024. Following this breakthrough performance, the band were added as main support on Psychedelic Porn Crumpets tour in April/May 2025, playing 22 shows & solidifying their reputation as one of the best live bands in the country. Their debut album 'Break You' (released in Nov 2023) is a great raw snapshot of young talented musicians creating something exciting, to-the-point, that leaves you replaying time & time again. New music is expected early 2026!

Title: Azamiah
Location:
Marlow
700 E. 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Primed to be the next group from the UK jazz underground to break out, Azamiah draw on 
jazz roots while touching on everything from James Blake’s production techniques to dubby 
low-end, taking the listener on a journey across moody and light landscapes.  Their 2023 
debut album ‘In Phases’ won praise from The Skinny and CLASH and received plays on BBC 
6Music and NTS.  A bridge between first album, and second, their recent EP ‘Two Lands’ 
released in May 2025 resonates with emotional honesty and is played with a fresh intensity. 
The band has played on stages across the UK and Europe including Latitude Festival, Jazz on Wick, Hidden Door, Edinburgh Jazz and Blues Festival, Brick Lane Jazz Festival, JAZZMI (IT) & North Sea Round Town (NL). Having recently completed their first UK headline tour in October 2025 to electric audiences in Glasgow, London, Bristol, Manchester, Leeds and Brighton and flooded with requests for a re-press of 'In Phases', a dedicated fanbase is making themselves known.

Title: SATALiGHTS
Location:
Zilker Brewing
1701 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702
Description: SATALiGHTS is an Austin-based band blending post-rock, ambient, and pop, known for albums like Little Star (2020), Veluga (2024), and Alebrijes (2025). Founded by Alexander Scott, the group has produced multiple EPs and singles, including "Sirenas," and is noted for its eclectic, space-themed soundscapes. They are on the verge of releasing their 5th studio album Ch1pT00nz on March 18th, 2026.

Title: P.H.0
Location:
The 13th Floor
711 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
P.H.0 [P-H-"zero"] is a NYC-based SILKPUNK quartet reshaping the city's underground. Fusing the raw, intense metal riffs with the haunting cry of the traditional Chinese erhu and techno-dystopian synths, they craft sounds that are at once chaotic and controlled, futuristic and ancient. Their live shows—referred to as "rituals"—transform ancient Taoist traditions into ear-crushing soundscapes that defy time, crashing into chaotic mosh pits under visceral strobing lights to create what some describe as a rave in an abandoned shrine from Cyberpunk 2077.

Their momentum has carried them from NYC festivals like New Colossus and Punk Island to sold-out shows across the East Coast, proving their reach in every environment—from the historic Gramercy Theatre, where they were handpicked by Danish prog-metal giants VOLA to support their North American tour, to inside an abandoned tunnel where they performed for more than 500 ravers. They were further recognized by NPR's Tiny Desk as a top 50 "Top Shelf" artist in 2025, and by Bandcamp Daily, who praised their debut album KARMA for “reimagining what Asian music might sound like.” P.H.0 continues to build a cult following, one ritual at a time.

Title: Money Mark
Location:
Shangri-La
1016 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702
Description: Legendary composer, inventor, and the unofficial “Fourth Beastie Boy,” Money Mark has been rewriting the sonic playbook since ’75. With a career spanning Billboard #1s and collaborations with icons like David Byrne, Jack White, Miley Cyrus, and Beck, he is the ultimate musical pivot point. Part virtuoso, part mad scientist, Mark currently spends his time inventing new instruments and using unconventional experiments to find the sounds of tomorrow.

Title: Luke Tyler Shelton
Location:
Las Perlas
405 E 7th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Born in the new millennium, Luke Tyler Shelton makes soul-soothing rock-and-roll with all the ineffable charm of a faraway time. With a profound gift for dreaming up heavenly melodies and fantastically loose grooves, the Los Angeles native found his sound as part of a close-knit scene of young musicians and artists—a DIY-minded community whose spirit and sensibilities recall the near-mythic creative hotbed of early-’70s Laurel Canyon. Produced by Shooter Jennings (Brandi Carlile, Tanya Tucker) and Jonathan Wilson (Father John Misty, Angel Olsen), Luke’s debut body of work merges country-rock, folk-pop, and Southern soul into songs that immediately transport the audience into a state of enchanted ease.

Title: Passion Pit
Location:
Inn Cahoots Austin Garden
1221 E. 6th St.
Austin TX 78702
Description: Passion Pit is an American alternative indie pop band formed in 2007 in Cambridge, Massachusetts, primarily acting as the moniker for singer-songwriter and producer Michael Angelakos. Initially a solo project, the band gained fame for, high-energy, synth-driven pop, with hits like "Sleepyhead" and "Take a Walk".

Title: very nice person
Location:
Shangri-La
1016 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702
Description: No description provided for film.

Title: Charley Crockett
Location:
Stubb’s
801 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Charley Crockett spent 15 years positioning himself firmly outside of the cogs and gears that
drive the country music industry. The path to stardom never interested the San Benito, Texas,
native. It was another path, the one forged by Willie Nelson and Waylon Jennings, which
captivated Crockett. “What is classified as solid country gold from Willie and Waylon now,”
Crockett says, “was absolutely what they saw as disruptive, not country, dangerous,
counterculture, whatever you want to call it.” Now, proudly in the crosshairs of those same
labels, Crockett is not just in his element, he’s thriving.
The April 3 release of Age of the Ram marks Crockett’s third studio album in just over a year
and third with Island Records. The LP completes The Sagebrush Trilogy, joining 2025’s The
Lonesome Drifter and the Grammy-nominated Dollar a Day records to paint a vivid picture of
Crockett’s journey from Texas dreamer, New York street singer, and twice-convicted felon to the
belly of the country music beast. Like the previous two, Age of the Ram was produced by
Shooter Jennings at the venerable Sunset Sound Studio 3 in Los Angeles.
In Jennings, he found a friend, a peer, and a producer with a grasp on Crockett’s approach to
music that most others missed. “They didn’t have a good handle on how I create and how fast I
can move when I’m inspired and the microphones are on,” he says.
After threading characters and storylines across the first two albums in the project, Age of the
Ram finds Crockett and Jennings pushing their creativity with a concept record echoing the
legacies of Nelson, Jennings, and even Gram Parsons. This is Crockett’s vision of hard country
music. Guitar and pedal steel are the rule on the album, often accompanied by piano, harp or
keys. The liner notes set the expectations from the start:
AGE OF THE RAM IS THE STORY OF BILLY MCLANE, A SMALL TIME CATTLE RUSTLER
WHO FINDS HIMSELF IN THE CROSSHAIRS OF THE SANTA FE RING. PURSUED BY
BOUNTY KILLERS WORKING FOR THE SHADOW SYNDICATE, BILLY MCLANE ESCAPES
INTO THE CRAZY MOUNTAINS WHERE THE OUTLAW WILL BECOME A LEGEND.
In McLane’s character, Age of the Ram puts a name to Sagebrush Trilogy characters like “The
Lonesome Drifter” and, time and again, threads Crockett’s life in music to the story he has
crafted. A series of short theme tracks — “The Life and Times of Billy McLane” which Crockett
penned, plus covers of “Rancho Deluxe,” Jimmy Buffett’s dueling themes for the 1975 cattle-
rustling comedy of the same name — frame the narrative. Throughout the album, sounds of
galloping horses, crackling old western movie dialogue and gunfights emerge over the blues,
bluegrass, waltzes and old-school two-step numbers. The sound may be vintage Crockett, but
his creativity is in overdrive, ensuring his spaghetti western tale shines through the music.
This is his version of Red Headed Stranger, but where Nelson’s definitive concept album
remained dark and shrouded in mystery, Crockett lays his vision bare. “Lonesome Dove”
reveals his numbness to artists chasing glory. “Kentucky Too Long” passes as a bank-robbery
song until Crockett deadpans “I’d have split the take if you really know me,” reminding listeners
of his loyalty to the people in his corner and evoking the heavy introspection of Parsons’
Grievous Angel record. Then, on “Billy McLane,” Crockett drives the concept home as he sings,
“I ain’t nobody, just another traveler, trying not to get killed by his name.”
Not until his cover of Billy Joe Shaver’s “Low Down Freedom” does Crockett break from his
characters and his concepts to pay tribute to late nights hanging with his producer — and a
lyrical cameo by Crockett’s wife and partner, Taylor Grace — on “Me & Shooter.” After all, that’s
still his right as the biggest thorn in Music City’s side.
“Any time a Texan goes into the Nashville machine and keeps his faculties,” Crockett says, “that
machine’s bound to change.”

Title: Victoryland
Location:
Hotel Vegas
Hotel Vegas at Volstead
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Victoryland is the Brooklyn-based project of musician Julian McCamman. His label-debut, My Heart Is A Room With No Cameras In It, out January 23, 2026 via Good English, is a triumphant, despondent, and ultimately fun experimental pop-rock album. Victoryland started in Philly 2023 with the release of tape, Sprain, which echoed more of what the Philly scene was dishing out: lo-fi, tape-recorded rock with a capital R. It was the collaborative efforts of producer Dan Howard that brought the new vision and sound into full focus.Dan and Julian previously worked on Julian’s now-defunct Texas-to-Philly based band, Blood’s Loving You Backwards LP, a collaborative effort between six bandmates. That experience pushed Julian toward a more personal process of recording, starting with home demos and then bringing them to Dan to shape into songs. This relationship ultimately led Julian to move to NYC.

The sound of the album, recorded between Julian’s Bed Stuy basement and Dan’s Williamsburg studio, is the sound of a bright and beaming collaboration. Landing somewhere between lo-fi and hi-fi production, the songs have the glossiness of a radio-friendly hit mixed with the distortion and rough edges of a home demo. Every song keeps at least one element of the initial recording, while building around the loops and half-songs Julian scrapped together. The lyrics on the record span from humorous self-aware popisms (“‘you and I’ will soon be, used to be ‘us’”) to crushing realities of bitterness(“i’ll never forgive you, that’s how I keep you close”) to abstract imagery (“mothers wave from doorways, in ostinato”), all delivered with such an immediacy and fervor that in the middle of singing along, you wonder if this guy slept much last night. 

My Heart wrangles with heavy subject matter (love, disconnection, sexual frustration, emptiness etc.), but the terminal statement of the record is about the cleansing nature of pop music. That, if a song can force you to “bliss out” over a hook or a loop, or even make you dance, it can be the spoonful of sugar to swallow the hard pill with; this is what Victoryland strives to do: package the most exhausting realities of life, love, and the search for connection in a world starved of it, into a fun 2-5 minutes, and for the runtime of Victoryland’s first major statement, you might even feel like you’re not alone.

Title: Karma Sheen
Location:
The 13th Floor
711 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Karma Sheen is a London-based project led by British-Pakistani artist Sameer Khan, fusing the spirit of psychedelic rock with the emotional depth of Hindustani Classical music. Born from the tension between tradition and distortion, the project reimagines raag-based forms through fuzz pedals, improvisation and diasporic imagination.
Rooted in Khan’s training under Ustaad Latafat Ali Khan (Sham Chaurasi Gharana), Karma Sheen translates classical ornamentation into immersive soundscapes. Khan is joined by guitarist Amad Chima, sitarist Rodrigo Bourganos, bassist and visual artist Grisha Grebbenikov, and drummer/DJ Arun Dhanjal, forming a collaboration that bridges ancient lineage with contemporary subculture.
The band released their debut album in 2023, followed by extensive UK and European touring, including sold-out headline shows at Rich Mix and the 100 Club, and festival appearances at Shambala and Trans Musicales, where they recorded a KEXP live session due for release in early 2026.
Now working with EBB Music, Karma Sheen are building international momentum as they prepare to release their second album in 2026.

Title: Nik Parr & The Selfless Lovers
Location:
Zilker Brewing
1701 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702
Description: Piano player, saxophonist and singer from Corpus Christi with a rock n' roll band. A nobody from nowhere. Band is Nik Parr & The Selfless Lovers. Been at it for ten years touring all over the country.

Title: very nice person
Location:
Mohawk
Mohawk Outdoor
912 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: No description provided for film.

Title: Ella Ion
Location:
Downright Austin
Global Stage @ Downright Austin Backyard
710 E. 10th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Emerging from Kaurna Land (Adelaide, Australia), fragmented-folk musician Ella Ion crafts music that cuts deep to the core. Drawing on influences of indie-rock, folk and country, her eloquent lyricism and masterful musical control delivers a cacophony of raw emotion, anchored by grit and heart. Ion has already amassed a loyal following across Australia, as well as listeners streaming her music across the UK, US, Europe and more. 

From intimate encounters of tender folk to electrifying post-rock arrangements, Ion flaunts her ability to transform a room of anyone and everyone into a fan. Ella Ion is holding nothing back as she barrels forward in a direction of raw sonic and personal exploration. Ion has supported Suki Waterhouse, Folk Bitch Trio, Jake Bugg, and Greg Mendez at shows across Australia, UK and Europe, as well as taking to the stage at The Great Escape Festival and Green Man Festival in the UK.

Title: CHALK
Location:
Downright Austin
Radio Day Stage @ Downright Austin Backyard
710 E. 10th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Chalk is the project of award-winning musicians and filmmaker
duo Ross Cullen and Benedict Goddard. An informed mix of
Dublin's guitar band scene and the sweaty hardcore dance
floors of their native Belfast, Chalk are IDLES meets Daniel Avery,
Death Grips meets Bicep - techno-infused industrial punk.
Since forming in 2022, Chalk have had a rise like no other,
quickly establishing themselves as an unmissable live act and
unpredictable, ferocious songwriters. Their debut UK/IRE tour
sold out, and led to further bookings across Europe as word of
their performance and guitar-melded techno spread via word of
mouth and support with national radio in the UK.
These songs were collated into the Conditions Trilogy, a family of
thirteen tracks that combine gritty Irish and German-influenced
techno and dance music with gothic, post-punk and jagged
guitar riffs and vocal melodies. This unique blend earned them
the support and kudos of their peers in Fontaines D.C., IDLES
and Sprints, all of whom handpicked the band to support them
throughout their tours.

Title: Samurai Drive
Location:
Elysium
705 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Samurai Drive are Daniel Riederauer and Soma Nove. Their music strives to create a cinematic sonic experience, reflecting elements of electronic, blues and new wave musical genres, combined with a dark film-noir aesthetic. Samurai Drive is best served driving down lonesome highways through the midnight hours.

Title: Joyeria
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Joyeria (pronounced Joy-ah-ree-ah) is a London-based outfit led by a Canadian-Polish songwriter who famously spent years quitting bands at their peak to keep his creative soul intact. Now a staple of London's underground scene, the group is a vehicle for "working in the dark"—a shape-shifting project that prizes sincerity over polish.

Musically, Joyeria delivers a darkly comic blend of slacker indie and experimental post-punk. Fronted by a deadpan baritone, the songs tackle the absurdity of modern adulthood, capitalism, and workday fatigue with biting, sardonic wit. Their EPs, FIM and Graceful Degradation, were produced by Dan Carey, captured raw and live-to-tape to preserve the "chaos" of the moment rather than a rehearsed demo.

While born from a singular vision, Joyeria is a formidable four-piece band. Known for being "purposely under-rehearsed," the ensemble thrives on improvisation, ensuring no two sets are the same. With a stage presence that balances Ian Curtis’s intensity with Stephen Malkmus’s irony, the band utilizes an unpredictable, dissonant energy to create a sound that is as imposing as it is eccentric.

Title: P.H.0
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
P.H.0 [P-H-"zero"] is a NYC-based SILKPUNK quartet reshaping the city's underground. Fusing the raw, intense metal riffs with the haunting cry of the traditional Chinese erhu and techno-dystopian synths, they craft sounds that are at once chaotic and controlled, futuristic and ancient. Their live shows—referred to as "rituals"—transform ancient Taoist traditions into ear-crushing soundscapes that defy time, crashing into chaotic mosh pits under visceral strobing lights to create what some describe as a rave in an abandoned shrine from Cyberpunk 2077.

Their momentum has carried them from NYC festivals like New Colossus and Punk Island to sold-out shows across the East Coast, proving their reach in every environment—from the historic Gramercy Theatre, where they were handpicked by Danish prog-metal giants VOLA to support their North American tour, to inside an abandoned tunnel where they performed for more than 500 ravers. They were further recognized by NPR's Tiny Desk as a top 50 "Top Shelf" artist in 2025, and by Bandcamp Daily, who praised their debut album KARMA for “reimagining what Asian music might sound like.” P.H.0 continues to build a cult following, one ritual at a time.

Title: Cure for Paranoia
Location:
Swan Dive
615 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Formed in the heart of Dallas but born on the highway, Cure for Paranoia came together during a “doomsday road trip” intended to be their last. Having recently been diagnosed with bipolar paranoid schizophrenia, frontman Cameron McCloud found clarity in a hot-boxed rental car; making music with collaborators Jay Analog and Tomahawk Jonez gave him peace of mind. “He said, ‘this is my cure for paranoia.’ Instantly, everyone’s eyes lit up,” a pivotal realization that cemented the group’s moniker. When the predicted earth-shattering meteor failed to hit, inspiration struck instead. As Jonez recalls, “On the way from Amarillo to Colorado Springs, like eight songs got written.”

Since then, Cure for Paranoia has shared bills with icons ranging from George Clinton & Parliament Funkadelic and Nas to Ludacris and Leon Bridges. Their resume includes stops at SXSW, Fortress Festival, and the NPR Tiny Desk Contest "On The Road" Tour, solidifying their status as festival favorites. While keen to distance themselves from standard regional production, they cultivated a signature "Trippy Soul infused hip hop" sound—a blend prioritized on their anchor project, the Work of A.R.T. EP.

Title: Hot Garbage
Location:
Hotel Vegas
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Hot Garbage melt into a heavily volatile yet undeniably palatable amalgamation of sonic elements. Calling on the driving rhythms of dark post-punk and motorik krautrock, the Toronto-based outfit seamlessly works shining melodies and swirling textures into deliberate, brooding arrangements. Since forming in 2015, the four-piece have toured extensively throughout Canada, the United States, Europe and the United Kingdom, bringing their intense live sets to festivals such as Levitation, Freakout Fest, Treefort, SXSW, Sled Island, FME, M for Montreal and Pop Montreal, and sharing the stage with notable acts such as Osees, Ty Segall, L.A. Witch, Frankie and the Witch Fingers, JJUUJJUU, Mdou Moctar, Wand and Kikagaku Moyo. The quartet recently returned with Precious Dream (January 19, 2024 via Mothland & Exag’ Records), a sprawling collection of songs written in isolation and brought together alongside visionary producer Graham Walsh (METZ, Preoccupations, Holy Fuck, !!!), offering an exhilarating new breath into the world of post-punk and modern psychedelia, a listening experience that is equally cathartic and introspective.

Title: Bikini Beach
Location:
Hotel Vegas
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: No description provided for film.

Title: The Haunted Youth
Location:
Elysium
705 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
The Haunted Youth is the band of Joachim Liebens and the biggest Belgian indie rock sensation of the last few years. After the release of the critically acclaimed and popular debut album Dawn of the Freak and touring almost non-stop, his sophomore album BOYS CRY TOO is set to release in May ‘26. Frontman Joachim Liebens blends dreamy guitars, nostalgic synths, and deeply personal lyrics into songs that, while dark in theme, offer comfort and connection. Since the release of their debut album Dawn of the Freak in November 2022, the band has toured relentlessly, playing to packed crowds from London to Madrid. Festivals like Rock Werchter, Pukkelpop, Lowlands, Sziget, and Iceland Airwaves eagerly welcomed them—The Haunted Youth came, played, and conquered.
2025 marks the beginning of the next chapter in The Haunted Youth’s compelling journey, with the release of their highly anticipated sophomore album BOYS CRY TOO, set for February 2026. The album signals a bold step forward in both sound and storytelling, pushing the band’s emotional and sonic boundaries even further.

Title: Cashier
Location:
Hotel Vegas
Hotel Vegas at Volstead
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: On their debut EP ‘The Weight,’ the Lafayette, Louisiana, four-piece reaches full definition, distilling themes of desire, insecurity, and disconnection into five tightly wound, melody-driven songs released by Julia’s War Recordings, the legendary Philly label founded by Doug Dulgarian of TAGABOW. Across the EP, bandleader Kylie Gaspard’s deeply direct vocal delivery cuts through dense riffs, grounding their songs with her gritty, emotional lyrics. Having recently shared stages with genre titans like Dinosaur Jr., Whirr, Nothing, and Narrow Head, Cashier are building a reputation for a massive sound that resonates far beyond their bayou home.

Title: Pierce Washington
Location:
Taco n Maiz
603 Sabine St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Pierce Washington is an award-winning hip-hop artist from Dallas, Texas, whose sound bridges jazz influences, live instrumentation, and modern production — creating music that feels both timeless and forward-thinking. Known for his cinematic storytelling and commanding stage presence, Pierce has emerged as one of the leading voices in Dallas hip-hop. His collaborations with legendary producer Madlib and rap icon Lil Wayne showcase his versatility and creative range. Fresh off headlining The Proof of Concept and Folktales tours, Pierce continues to push artistic boundaries through his innovative approach to music, visuals, and brand artistry.

Title: Next of Kin
Location:
Rivian Electric Roadhouse
208 S Congress Ave.
Austin TX 78704
Description:
Next of Kin is an alt-country trio with a sound that blends country-tinged soundscapes, rich three-part harmonies, and honest storytelling. 

In just over a year, what started as a chance collaboration between three solo artists transformed into much more – a budding new band, a full album, an ACL Festival debut. 

Some call it fate, others luck – they call it Next of Kin.

Title: Flesh Juicer
Location:
Hotel Vegas
Hotel Vegas Patio
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Flesh Juicer is Taiwan’s most iconic heavy metal band, formed in 2006 in Taichung. Blending Deathcore and Hardcore with electronic textures and traditional pentatonic scales, they’ve forged a distinctive Taiwanese metal sound rooted in local culture. Their music draws inspiration from folk religion, temple processions, and social realities, transforming raw energy into a visceral expression of contemporary Taiwan.
Flesh Juicer’s work has consistently earned critical acclaim both in Taiwan and internationally. Their debut album ‘GIGO’ (2015) won the Golden Indie Music Award for Best Rock Album, while ‘Deep Sea Fairy Tale’ (2018) earned nominations at the AIM Independent Music Awards（US）. In 2021, ‘GOLDEN Taizi BRO’ swept three GIMA trophies, Best Live Performance, Best Band, and Best Album, solidifying their status as Taiwan’s metal powerhouse.

Known for massive sold-out shows of over 5,000 fans, Flesh Juicer has brought their electrifying performances to Clockenflap (Hong Kong), Summer Sonic (Japan), and Hammer Sonic (Indonesia). With a new album set for release in 2026 and a North American tour in the works, Flesh Juicer is not only a flagship band in Taiwan’s metal scene but also demonstrates strong potential to make a lasting impact on the global stage.

Title: haha Laughing
Location:
Hotel Vegas
Hotel Vegas Patio
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: haha Laughing is an Austin-based experimental noise rap project consisting of long-time local musicians Jay Dilick and Aby Oviedo. The duo blends elements of hardcore rap, free-jazz, distorted electronic music and punk energy into 3 minute explosions of catchy catharsis. The chaos they curate is driven home through their live shows where Dilick and Oviedo's back and forth vocals, noise breakdowns, sample flips, and sense of playfulness create a must see live performance that is energetically engaging and delightfully disorienting. haha Laughing has released multiple EP's and singles since 2021, and released their first album "LOSING TEETH DREAM" in May of 2025. Their follow up EP "BIG WHATEVER" is set to be released Spring 2026. haha Live. haha Laugh. haha Love.

Title: FEET
Location:
Hotel Vegas
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
When FEET self-released their debut album in 2019, the group offered up a particularly oblique title: What’s Inside Is More Than Just Ham. But once listeners delved into the record,  they found sloshing’s of guitar music that careered wildly between indie, psych, and subtle shades of new wave across an exciting and unpredictable 35 minutes. Since then, FEET have seen prominent live shows have seen them supporting the likes of Inhaler and even The Rolling Stones at their huge 2022 Hyde Park shows. The bands second album "Make It Up" garnered further critical acclaim with the band being invited to perform a BBC 6Music Live session off the back of the single releases Greasy Boy, Why Would I Lie and The Real Thing. 

The band are returning in 2026 with their third album to be released on Submarine Cat Records amidst a year of heavy touring which will see the band on two UK and European headline stints throughout the album campaign. A very welcome return, then, for one of Britain’s most inventive bands, starting a new chapter afresh that builds on the brilliance of their first outing and takes it to a new level entirely. 2026, it’s fair to say, is ready for Feet to stomp all over it.

Title: Buddy Red
Location:
Stubb’s
801 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Buddy Red is the Southern blues artist delivering the raw, cinematic sound of the Black American experience. A vocalist, guitarist, and preacher of truth, he stands where the cosmic mystique of Jimi Hendrix meets the uncompromising funk of James Brown and the psychedelic scope of Pink Floyd. A fixture of Atlanta's legendary Northside Tavern, Buddy is a vital voice recognized for his visceral guitar work and impassioned storytelling, captivating live and digital audiences alike.

Since his breakout debut at SXSW, which included a featured set at The Shade Room's activation in 2023, his momentum is undeniable. He recently performed at the James Brown Estate for the Black Music Action Coalition’s Juneteenth celebration and shared the stage with Little Richard's original band at Tutti Frutti Music Festival. His new single, "Sold His Soul," cemented his rise as a cinematic blues revivalist, with his cultural influence extending through partnerships with the Atlanta Hawks and BMW and a festival performance at Neon Prarie in Tulsa. Buddy Red is returning to SXSW as an essential force.

Title: TTSSFU
Location:
Hotel Vegas
Hotel Vegas Patio
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: TTSSFU – aka Tasmin Nicole Stephens – quickly went from being a mere fixture in Manchester’s indie scene to one of its fastest-rising stars, now poised to break out well beyond it. Known for conjuring vast, atmospheric worlds from her bedroom using just Garageband, she watched as early releases like her Warhol-inspired 2024 EP “Me, Jed and Andy” picked up steam. Since then, she’s traded bedrooms for stages supporting Kim Deal, English Teacher, Soccer Mommy and Mannequin Pussy, plus sold out headlines across the UK and packed festival sets from Green Man to the Great Escape.

Title: The Haunted Youth
Location:
Hotel Vegas
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
The Haunted Youth is the band of Joachim Liebens and the biggest Belgian indie rock sensation of the last few years. After the release of the critically acclaimed and popular debut album Dawn of the Freak and touring almost non-stop, his sophomore album BOYS CRY TOO is set to release in May ‘26. Frontman Joachim Liebens blends dreamy guitars, nostalgic synths, and deeply personal lyrics into songs that, while dark in theme, offer comfort and connection. Since the release of their debut album Dawn of the Freak in November 2022, the band has toured relentlessly, playing to packed crowds from London to Madrid. Festivals like Rock Werchter, Pukkelpop, Lowlands, Sziget, and Iceland Airwaves eagerly welcomed them—The Haunted Youth came, played, and conquered.
2025 marks the beginning of the next chapter in The Haunted Youth’s compelling journey, with the release of their highly anticipated sophomore album BOYS CRY TOO, set for February 2026. The album signals a bold step forward in both sound and storytelling, pushing the band’s emotional and sonic boundaries even further.

Title: Agatha is Dead!
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Formed in the lockdown winter of 2021, Agatha Is Dead! began as a post-punk experiment between longtime friends Lilly and Noah, who first made music together in their school days. Their shared love for Joy Division, The Cure, Molchat Doma, and Fontaines D.C. laid the foundation for their darkly urgent sound. With the addition of drummer Joey Ramone Hansen−bringing grunge and pop−punk bite−and Arthur on bass, the band played their first shows in 2022 and quickly carved out a place in Berlin's underground scene.

The band signed to Berlin label Duchess Box Records in May 2025 and will release their debut EP "order" in on 10th October.

The first singles drew attention from big online magazines such as Post-Punk.com and Diffus as well as major radio station airplay and big playlist support from Spotify and Apple Music.

This September, the band will embark on their first-ever headline tour with shows in Berlin and Leipzig selling out in advance., marking a new chapter in their journey. Their Berlin show at Privatclub sold out an incredible five months in advance, underscoring the growing excitement surrounding the band.

Drawing further inspiration from The Pretty Reckless, DIIV, Liily, and Bloc Party, Agatha Is Dead! have honed a sound that is heavy, hypnotic, and deeply emotional. With their debut EP Order on the horizon, the band are staking their claim in the next wave of alternative rock−grabbing hearts, shaking stages, and refusing to go unnoticed.

Title: Glaze
Location:
Mohawk
Mohawk Indoor
912 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Austin-based four-piece Glaze delivers a heavy, hook-driven take on alternative shoegaze. Armed with distorted, catchy guitar lines and a reputation for captivating live shows, the band has steadily won over audiences from Vancouver to Monterrey. Following a more recent East coast tour opening for Mass of the Fermenting Dregs, Glaze is bringing their road-tested energy back home for SXSW. The band is currently setting the stage for the release of their debut LP, with plans to release in Summer 2026.

Title: Spacestation
Location:
Inn Cahoots
Inn Cahoots Studio
1221 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78702

Title: The Bures Band
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
THE BURES BAND BIO 

The Bures Band, is a fresh and classic mix of American folk rock and blues, driven by
Stephen Bailey (guitar and vocals) recognised for his work as a solo artist and for his role
as lead vocals and keys in psychedelic rock outfit, Mt. Mountain (Fuzz Club UK)) who have
shared the stage with greats including: King Gizzard & the Lizard Wizard, Thee Oh Sees,
Moon Duo, The Murlocs, Orb, Sleep, The Babe Rainbow) and showcases the musical
talents of local musicians: Callum Anderson (lead guitar and vocals) Noah Symmans
(drums) Danny Miles (guitar and vocals) and Tom
Beech (bass) 

Bookings For AUS/NZ: paul@billionsbooking.com.au
pat@billions.com.au

Title: Nervous
Location:
Valhalla
710 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: nervous is an independent band from Austin Texas colored in a mix of Emo, Rock, and Americana that creates a unique "yallternative" sound.

Title: PONS
Location:
Hotel Vegas
1502 E 6th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
IF YOU STOP YOU DIE - 

Rock n’ Roll will never be a lazy game. PONS is here to set the record straight.

Channeling the wild, untamable spirit of the masters of the road that came before them, PONS brings a relentless, “NO MERCY” approach to DIY touring. Burning more calories in one night than most bands do in a year, PONS doesn’t play just music—they detonate on stage

Taking cues from the anarchic swagger of Iggy Pop and the electronic chaos of Suicide, frontperson Jack Parker spits the digital bile of our decaying, internet-saturated world straight in your face. It’s a new noise pop for the modern age.

With their dashing look and salesman’s eye, PONS has supported notable acts such as Gogol Bordello, Lambrini Girls, A Place To Bury Strangers, Laura Jane Grace, Geese, Vundabar, Frankie and the Witch Fingers, and more. 

BEAR WITNESS TO PONS.

Title: Alanis Morissette
Location:
Stubb’s
801 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701

Title: Hot Garbage
Location:
Downright Austin
Radio Day Stage @ Downright Austin Backyard
710 E. 10th St.
Austin TX 78701
Description: Hot Garbage melt into a heavily volatile yet undeniably palatable amalgamation of sonic elements. Calling on the driving rhythms of dark post-punk and motorik krautrock, the Toronto-based outfit seamlessly works shining melodies and swirling textures into deliberate, brooding arrangements. Since forming in 2015, the four-piece have toured extensively throughout Canada, the United States, Europe and the United Kingdom, bringing their intense live sets to festivals such as Levitation, Freakout Fest, Treefort, SXSW, Sled Island, FME, M for Montreal and Pop Montreal, and sharing the stage with notable acts such as Osees, Ty Segall, L.A. Witch, Frankie and the Witch Fingers, JJUUJJUU, Mdou Moctar, Wand and Kikagaku Moyo. The quartet recently returned with Precious Dream (January 19, 2024 via Mothland & Exag’ Records), a sprawling collection of songs written in isolation and brought together alongside visionary producer Graham Walsh (METZ, Preoccupations, Holy Fuck, !!!), offering an exhilarating new breath into the world of post-punk and modern psychedelia, a listening experience that is equally cathartic and introspective.

Title: Tokyo Joshi Pro Wrestling TEXAS STAMPEDE -March 2026
Location:
Palmer Events Center
900 Barton Springs Rd.
Austin TX 78704
Description:
For the first time ever, Tokyo Joshi Pro Wrestling arrives in Austin — and makes its SXSW debut.
TJPW Texas Stampede is a high-energy tri-city tour across Austin, Dallas, and Houston, featuring 18 star wrestlers from Tokyo Joshi Pro Wrestling, Japan’s premier all-women’s promotion — marking one of the largest international showcases in women’s pro-wrestling history. 

Driven by the motto “Kawaii × Dream × Strong,” TJPW delivers a one-of-a-kind entertainment experience where pop-idol charm meets world-class athleticism. Nowhere else will you see a performer singing to the crowd one moment and launching a perfectly timed dropkick the next. 

This super-happy, family-friendly cultural event welcomes fans of all ages — bring your friends, bring your kids, and experience the excitement together.
At TJPW, All Women Are Heroes. 

While SXSW badge holders are eligible for complimentary admission (first-come, first-served), those who want to select their preferred seating and secure the best views can take advantage of an exclusive offer just for SXSW attendees: 
EXTRA SXSW Offer — 25% Off 
Use code SXSWInsider25 at checkout to receive 25% off your ticket purchase. 

Day 1 – March 17 
Secure your tickets here: 
https://tokyostoryusa.com/products/tjpw-austin-2026 

Day 2 – March 18 
Secure your tickets here: 
https://tokyostoryusa.com/products/tjpw-texas-stampede-in-austin-day-2

Location:
Stubb’s
801 Red River St.
Austin TX 78701
Description:
Hermanos Gutiérrez is a two-piece band formed of the brothers Alejandro and Estevan Gutiérrez.
Taking influence from 1950's Latin America Sound they transfer the listeners to journeys through beautiful landscapes.
